description: A 'wesoma' relation runs in more than one direction among its participants. Mutual trust means each trusts the other; mutual aid places each person within both giving and receiving, though the contributions need not match at every moment. 'wiso' (RECP) marks participants acting on one another inside a clause, while 'wesoma' qualifies the relation itself. 'nuawe' (together) requires only shared participation.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then unround and move the tongue forward into true-mid front 'e'. Raise the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's' and direct air through its central groove into stressed true-mid back rounded 'o'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the resonance through the nose, and open into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The opening and sibilant middle echo 'wiso' (RECP) and 'wisola' (exchange). That audible family can help keep Phi's reciprocal relations together in memory.
usage_notes: The adjective can qualify a relational noun, and its noun use is mutuality. When plural participants act on one another inside a clause, 'wiso' usually carries the reciprocal syntax.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Tool libraries and mutual-aid circles depend on people entering both sides of provision over time. 'wesoma' names that reciprocal structure without requiring identical contributions.

semantic_domains.community: reciprocity among people in a shared relationship
